text.title: Dependency Spike Kill-Criteria Template Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the Dependency Spike Kill-Criteria Template methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the last rule binds the decision tree skip path.

rule.statement: All kill-criteria MUST be written and time-stamped BEFORE spike code is touched. Post-hoc criteria are rejected as rationalisation.
rule.rationale: Sunk-cost-fallacy prevention; pre-commitment is the only mechanism that resists motivated reasoning.

rule.statement: Every criterion declares an operator + threshold + measurement source (e.g. `bundle_size_delta_kb < 50 measured via webpack-bundle-analyzer`). Vague criteria ('feels heavy') are rejected.
Spec requires ≥3 criteria spanning at least two of: bundle, perf, API ergonomics, license, maintenance signal, security. Single-axis criteria miss obvious failure modes.
rule.rationale: Multi-axis evaluation prevents tunnel-vision rejection or adoption.

rule.statement: When the time-box expires, the operator MUST record verdict ∈ {kill, continue, adopt} with one-line rationale. 'Need more time' is not a verdict.
rule.rationale: open-ended spikes consume more than they discover.

rule.statement: License compatibility is a non-negotiable criterion; AGPL/SSPL/proprietary licenses kill adoption unless legal pre-approved.
rule.rationale: Legal exposure cannot be retroactively unwound.

rule.statement: Maintenance signal (last release date, open issue ratio, single-maintainer risk) is a non-negotiable criterion.
rule.rationale: Abandoned deps become tech debt the day the maintainer ghosts.

rule.statement: When the decision tree routes to `skip-this-methodology`, the agent MUST emit a structured `skip` decision (with reason + suggested-sibling) rather than running Dependency Spike Kill-Criteria Template under degraded conditions.
rule.rationale: decision-tree contract. The decision tree is the only place that knows the routing; skipping must be observable so the orchestrator can log the routed-elsewhere path.
